title: Pro Football Reference

infobox.value: (**PFR**) is an online statistics database for professional [American football](/wiki/American_football "American football") maintained by [Sports Reference](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference"). The site provides career statistics for players, teams, and games, as well as records and [NFL draft](/wiki/NFL_draft "NFL draft") history.[[1]](#cite_note-1) PFR was established independently by Doug Drinen in 2000, and became part of Sports Reference in 2007. Sports Reference also publishes similar statistics websites for basketball, [baseball](/wiki/Baseball_Reference "Baseball Reference"), and hockey.[[2]](#cite_note-CBS_News-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American football statistics database

Pro Football Reference

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Available in | English |
| Owner | [Sports Reference](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference") |
| URL | [www.pro-football-reference.com](https://www.pro-football-reference.com/) |
| Launched | December 2000; 25 years ago (2000-12) |
| Current status | Online |

**Pro Football Reference** (**PFR**) is an online statistics database for professional [American football](/wiki/American_football "American football") maintained by [Sports Reference](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference"). The site provides career statistics for players, teams, and games, as well as records and [NFL draft](/wiki/NFL_draft "NFL draft") history.[[1]](#cite_note-1) PFR was established independently by Doug Drinen in 2000, and became part of Sports Reference in 2007. Sports Reference also publishes similar statistics websites for basketball, [baseball](/wiki/Baseball_Reference "Baseball Reference"), and hockey.[[2]](#cite_note-CBS_News-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pro_Football_Reference&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Pro Football Reference website and database was established by Doug Drinen in December 2000.[[4]](#cite_note-about-4) In 2007, PFR merged with the previously unrelated [Baseball Reference](/wiki/Baseball_Reference "Baseball Reference") and Basketball Reference to form [Sports Reference](/wiki/Sports_Reference "Sports Reference").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[4]](#cite_note-about-4) In December 2019, PFR introduced the Pro Football Hall of Fame monitor. The purpose of this monitor is to apply a formula to quantify player contributions during their career (including [All-Pro](/wiki/All-Pro "All-Pro") selections, [Pro Bowl](/wiki/Pro_Bowl "Pro Bowl") selections, various awards, and career statistics) and to highlight where [Pro Football Hall of Fame](/wiki/Pro_Football_Hall_of_Fame "Pro Football Hall of Fame") players rank in comparison, often with the goal of estimating a player's chances of making the Hall of Fame.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8) Drinen is also responsible for the creation of the "approximate value" statistic, a metric used to quantify the estimated value of a player's performance based on individual playing time and awards as well as team production in a given season.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In 2000, John Turney and Nick Webster, members of the [Professional Football Researchers Association](/wiki/Professional_Football_Researchers_Association "Professional Football Researchers Association") (PFRA), conducted extensive research to create a more complete record of sacks in the NFL. After examining the play-by-play records of every NFL team as well as game film at NFL films they compiled a list of players with 100 sacks beginning in 1960.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) These statistics, both the official and unofficial sack counts, are counted and displayed separately on the site from 1960 onwards.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
